phases.name: Detect DependencyManifestPinned from a committed Cargo.lock

steps.name: content_exists probe + RepoFacts.cargo_lock_url + DependencyManifestPinned arm
steps.description: Add a content_exists effect (GET /repos/{o}/{r}/contents/Cargo.lock; 200->present with the file html_url, 404->absent, other->error) in adapter-github; extend the pure RepoFacts with cargo_lock_url: Option<String>; add the DependencyManifestPinned arm to detect_signals (fires when cargo_lock_url is Some). harvest_repo probes contents/Cargo.lock and assembles the fuller RepoFacts, so a real repo with a committed Cargo.lock now yields the org.openlore.philosophy.dependency-pinning candidate.
criteria: RGSD-2 happy: a repo whose contents/Cargo.lock is 200 derives the dependency-pinning candidate (scrape_repo_with_a_committed_cargo_lock_derives_the_dependency_pinning_candidate)
RGSD-2 guardrail: a repo whose contents/Cargo.lock is 404 proposes NO dependency-pinning candidate (scrape_repo_without_a_cargo_lock_proposes_no_dependency_pinning_candidate stays green)
detection stays pure + presence-gated; confidence stays the mapping default 0.25
RGSD-1 memory-safety detection + the existing scrape suite stay green (union bridge, default-404 for unconfigured contents)
steps.implementation_notes: PURE (scraper-domain/detect.rs): (1) extend RepoFacts with `pub cargo_lock_url: Option<String>` (the committed Cargo.lock's file html_url when present; None when absent). Update any RepoFacts construction sites (RGSD-1's parse_repo_facts builds the body-derived fields — default cargo_lock_url to None there and let harvest_repo fill it, OR have harvest assemble the full struct). (2) In detect_signals, ADD the DependencyManifestPinned arm ALONGSIDE the existing MemorySafetyLanguage arm: when facts.cargo_lock_url is Some(url), push Signal{ kind: SignalKind::DependencyManifestPinned, value: "Cargo.lock committed (pinned dependencies)".to_string(), source_url: url.clone() }. Both arms are independent; a repo can fire both. EFFECT (adapter-github/lib.rs): (3) add an async method content_exists(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str, path: &str) -> Result<Option<String>, GithubError>: GET {api_base}/repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}; on 2xx parse the body and return Ok(Some(html_url)) (read the file `html_url`; fall back to a constructed blob URL if absent); on 404 return Ok(None) (absent is NOT an error); on other statuses map to the SAME GithubError classification get_public uses (rate-limit 403 -> RateLimited, 401 -> TokenRejected, network -> Network). Reuse get_public's client-build + auth-header logic — factor a shared helper if it reduces duplication, or add a sibling method. (4) In harvest_repo, after building the body-derived RepoFacts, call content_exists(owner, repo, "Cargo.lock")? and set facts.cargo_lock_url before detect_signals. Keep the RGSD-1 union with parse_signals. Keep harvest_user unchanged. client.rs: adjust parse_repo_facts / add a small pure helper if you parse the contents body there. Run cargo run -p xtask -- check-arch (stays 21). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): DELIVER RED_UNIT — proptest/example unit tests for the pure seams: detect_signals fires DependencyManifestPinned iff cargo_lock_url.is_some() (and independently of the language arm); RepoFacts with Some/None cargo_lock_url; a parse helper for the contents html_url if added. content_exists's 200/404/other mapping is an adapter concern — cover it with an adapter-github integration/unit test against a stub if practical, else via the acceptance path. Example-based is documented FALLBACK. The acceptance scenarios are pre-authored by DISTILL — do NOT rewrite them.

validation.reviewer: orchestrator (authored from the committed DESIGN doc + SPIKE) + automated quality gate
validation.review_verdict: APPROVED — automated quality gate PASS (single-step slice, 1 genuine RED owned + 1 GREEN-today guardrail, ratio 1 step / 3 production files = 0.33, step ID valid, criteria <=5). Specified by the RGSD design §1/§5 + the SPIKE (contents/Cargo.lock 200=present/404=absent, body carries file html_url); the Phase-4 adversarial implementation review remains the substantive gate.
